10.response: {"Q0":"I did not understand why there was not keyboard navigation. I remember worrying about trying to tab over from the message response box to the continue button. I could not see any indication that the button was selected and I did not want to try out pressing space/enter for fear it would end the game. Switching between the mouse and the j/k/l position with the right hand was less than ideal. Though not detrimental to my performance. I do not believe a change would have lead to a difference in score. \n\nThis has nothing to do with the instructions, but I am not sure if there is going to be a comments section and I wanted to get this in. "}

11.response: {"Q0":"Gambling performance over time maybe. Something with response times in the j/k/l keys. "}

13.response: {"Q0":"Excellent graphics. I noticed two typos, one in the instructions of the game and one in the survey. I do not remember the exact instances, but they were both less than 8 letters and off by only 1. Something like shbould instead of should.  It did not interfere with the clarity of the assignment. I would have liked a harder planet pattern. Something like rock+dirt+rock+wood+rock+dirt and a little variety. The same pattern left plenty for the competition seeker in me. "}

0.response: {"Q0":"I applied pressure with  my tongue to either the roof of my mouth or the bottom depending on what my intended galaxy selection was. The slight discomfort was a helper to stay focused. I do not believe that I did this necessarily for all rounds. But there was a handful of times that I could feel myself begin to slip or when I accidentally used my j/k/l keys to respond to the a/s prompt. Aside from that, it was a lot of muscle memory and a strategy that gave slightly higher preference to riskier and more rewarding lotteries when I felt there was an even split between the options. "}

7.response: {"Q0":"I do not believe so. Both gems were still for sale and so I had no reason to avoid them. I did make the choice to avoid the green gems in the meteor shower after the blue became no longer for sale. I felt the chance of a green turning into a gem was not enough to cover the potential win of 10 gold (20 sale - 10 click)"}

9.response: {"Q0":"I definitely had to place more attention on the galaxy selection because the timer was more limited and required an active choice and key response. The blue/red responses had room for error and the patterns did not change in the excavation, or at least I do not remember a change. dirt,rock,wood and rock,dirt,wood. So either <l><j><k> or <j><l><k>. My responses provided enough wiggle room with the timings and the cost of an excessive action was not high enough to worry about. "}

1.response: {"Q0":"Not that I recall. But I should emphasize that the tongue trick was not used on every round. It was more of a temporary utility if I found myself distracted, or when I needed a distracted such as if I could sense that I was going to sneeze or had an itch. It was more willy-nilly than systematic. "}

2.response: {"Q0":"I felt the outcomes were less than half of the time. My best guess in the order of how likely I believe they are possible: 4/10, 3/10, 5/10. "}

8.response: {"Q0":"Best value. When I felt the values were tied, or very close, I often went with the higher reward. I also feel that I went with the higher reward when the alternative option was less than $3. A small reward was not all too interesting and I was willing to take a risk. But I still tried to keep the risks reasonable. I avoided anything I felt was less than a 15%. "}

3.response: {"Q0":"Yes. There was only one message and it was easy to identify. It also came towards the end and so it was very easy to adapt to because I felt quite adept at the game by then. "}

4.response: {"Q0":"completely. But it was not clear if the astronaut would ever come back at a later date. I felt it was possible maybe he was going home to regain a quick sense of companionship. I came to realize he would be gone for good, but this could be better emphasized. I suppose it is implicitly stated in that there was no message about his return. "}

12.response: {"Q0":"I feel that one or two of my a/s misses was due to a lag, but that might just be my way of convincing myself that I was not too slow in my thinking. With the limited response times, I am not sure if anything could be done about this. 10/10 A+ otherwise. "}
